entry.title: Optimized Header Sync
entry.summary:
In the discussion about blockchain verification and efficiency, a suggestion was made to implement checkpoints every 2016 blocks, incorporating the corresponding bits value for that block set. This approach aims to optimize the process by which nodes verify the amount of work their chain has undergone, requiring only a transmission of approximately 10kB of data under current conditions. The proposal outlines that by doing so, one could validate changes in each node's chain target by downloading the headers between these checkpoints, which would occupy about 80kB with the suggested compact encoding.
The method also accounts for potential hardforks that might allow for more frequent difficulty adjustments. Even in such scenarios, assigning a bits value at every 2016th block ensures a baseline for the minimum work required for subsequent blocks within that segment. Although this might lead to a less precise estimate of total work done compared to the current system, it still facilitates an effective way to gauge the computational effort expended across the network. This strategy not only simplifies the verification process but also enhances the efficiency of data transmission among nodes, making the blockchain more scalable and manageable.
